seoTitle: How to Use Trello for Team Project Management
metaDescription: Set up Trello for cross-functional team projects, with structured workflow lists, Butler automation, and dashboard tracking views.
intro: Managing a team's sprints and deliverables gets messy fast without a shared source of truth. This guide covers structuring a Trello board specifically for team project management, including workflow lists, automation, and tracking tools.

steps.heading: Create a shared team board
steps.body: Set up a new board, name your project, and invite your team collaborators from the start.

steps.heading: Build a team workflow structure
steps.body: Lay out core lists such as Resources, To Do, Blocked, and Done to reflect how your team actually works.

steps.heading: Build out a detailed task card
steps.body: Add a rich description and a multi-tier subtask checklist to your master task cards.

steps.heading: Assign owners and set priorities
steps.body: Use the Members option to delegate task owners, and apply high-contrast labels to flag urgent deliverables.

steps.heading: Set up Butler automation
steps.body: Configure automation scripts to automatically assign owners or adjust priority labels when cards move between lists.

steps.heading: Use the Dashboard view to track progress
steps.body: Switch to the Dashboard view to monitor team workloads and spot bottlenecks across the project.

faq.question: What lists should a team project board have?
faq.answer: A common structure includes Resources, To Do, Blocked, and Done, but you can adjust based on your team's actual process.

faq.question: Can Butler automation handle repetitive team tasks?
faq.answer: Yes, Butler can automatically assign owners or adjust labels based on triggers like a card moving to a new list.

faq.question: How do I keep track of team communication on a card?
faq.answer: Use @mentions in card comments to centralize discussion and keep everyone updated in context.

faq.question: Can I connect other communication tools to my team board?
faq.answer: Yes, Power-Ups like Slack or Google Drive can be added to keep your project aligned with your other tools.
